author: draganrakita
content: Can you explain what private withdrawal is?

author: lightclient
content:
Users want eth that is not linked to their identity. The standard way for this today is to use a privacy protocol where you deposit, wait / do some operations in private, then withdraw. To withdraw today, you need ETH. This becomes a chicken-and-egg problem, because having eth in another wallet not anonymously would defeat the purpose. So then you need to use a centralized provider to relay your withdrawal which will leak your information and make you reliant on a centralized party.

Frame tx let's you submit these transactions natively because you can do the withdrawal first then pay for the gas

author: draganrakita
content: This looks like as a main motivation for frame tx. I would like to see more on how this is done, and what mechanism would we implement if we dont have evm execution in the mix.
